changesProposed.name: DCVC

verified.name: DCVC
verified.thesis: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors. The firm invests in companies solving trillion-dollar problems at the intersection of computational approaches and AI. Investment areas include AI, climate, engineering, materials science, robotics, space, water, biology, and defense and security.
verified.notes: Founded year cannot be verified from sources. Source [1] states 'For 15 years, DCVC has backed brilliant entrepreneurs' and mentions '13 funds to date' and 'six flagship funds' but does not specify a founding year. The database entry lists 2011 but this is not supported by the provided sources.

changes.current: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors. The firm invests in companies solving trillion-dollar problems at the intersection of computational approaches and data. Investment areas include AI, climate, engineering, materials science, robotics, space, water, biology, and defense.

changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage deep tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing. In 2023, the investment fund separated from The Engine nonprofit incubator.
verified.notes: Engine Ventures is the venture capital arm that spun out from The Engine nonprofit in 2023. The Engine remains a separate 501(c)(3) nonprofit incubator and accelerator. Wikipedia article is about The Engine organization (nonprofit); Engine Ventures is the VC firm. AUM not explicitly stated in sources. Key partners (Katie Rae, Reed Sturtevant) not confirmed in provided sources. Flagship fund name not confirmed in sources.

changesProposed.name: General Catalyst

verified.name: General Catalyst
verified.thesis: General Catalyst invests across seed, venture, and growth stages. The firm backs founders and invests in critical institutions, with focus on applied AI and global resiliency.
verified.notes: In June 2024, General Catalyst acquired La Famiglia (German VC firm), after which Jeannette zu Fürstenberg became a managing director overseeing European business. In October 2024, firm raised $8 billion in new funds including $4.5 billion for core venture funds. Neeraj Arora listed in database entry but not found in current sources as current senior partner.

changes.current: General Catalyst is an investment and transformation company that backs founders across seed, venture, and growth stages. The firm partners with entrepreneurs to build toward global resiliency and applied AI, originating new companies from scratch and transforming critical institutions.
changes.verified: General Catalyst invests across seed, venture, and growth stages. The firm backs founders and invests in critical institutions, with focus on applied AI and global resiliency.

changesProposed.notes: In June 2024, General Catalyst acquired La Famiglia (German VC firm), after which Jeannette zu Fürstenberg became a managing director overseeing European business. changesProposed.name: Lightspeed Venture Partners

verified.name: Lightspeed Venture Partners
verified.thesis: For over twenty years Lightspeed has been the first investor and an early backer of some of the most innovative companies in the world. The firm stands behind companies with high conviction from Seed to Series F and beyond.
verified.notes: Founded year 2000 cannot be verified from provided sources; sources only state 'over twenty years' as of publication date. AUM figure from database entry ($9B+ Dec 2025) cannot be verified from provided sources. HQ location (Menlo Park, CA) cannot be verified from provided sources. Sector focus cannot be verified from provided sources. No explicit current flagship fund name found in sources. Team page [2] lists 48 investors and 21 venture/operating partners; listed key_partners are those explicitly titled as Partner or Co-Founder on the firm website.

changes.current: Lightspeed invests in companies from Seed to Series F and beyond with high conviction. The firm has been a first investor and early backer of innovative companies over more than twenty years.

changesProposed.name: Shield Capital

verified.name: Shield Capital
verified.thesis: Shield Capital is a venture capital firm investing in early-stage companies building technologies that matter in artificial intelligence, autonomy, cybersecurity, and space. The firm's veteran team of founders, investors, and national security leaders focuses on the convergence of commercial technology and national security.

changesProposed.name: Thrive Capital Management, LLC

current.name: Thrive Capital Management, LLC
current.hq: New York City, New York, U.S.

verified.name: Thrive Capital Management, LLC
verified.hq: New York City, New York, U.S.
verified.thesis: Thrive Capital is an American venture capital firm that focuses on software and internet investments. The firm invests heavily in fewer companies to provide closer relationships with founders and greater visibility into portfolio companies. It simultaneously maintains participation across early-stage, growth, and late-stage investments.
verified.notes: Nitin Nohria became first executive chairman in 2022. Thrive Capital Partners IX raised $5B in 2024. As of 2026, firm has raised $22.3B total and manages $50B+. Thrive Capital Partners X raised $10B+ in 2026.

changes.current: Thrive Capital is an American venture capital firm that focuses on software and internet investments. The firm invests across early-stage, growth, and late-stage companies while maintaining a strategy of investing heavily in fewer companies to provide closer relationships with founders and greater visibility into portfolio companies.
changes.verified: Thrive Capital is an American venture capital firm that focuses on software and internet investments. The firm invests heavily in fewer companies to provide closer relationships with founders and greater visibility into portfolio companies. It simultaneously maintains participation across early-stage, growth, and late-stage investments.
